
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(pheatmap)


# LOAD EXAMPLE DATA

fid <- read_csv( "example_fid_results.csv" )
oracle <- read_csv(
  "example_oracle_metrics.csv"
)


# FID SUMMARY

fid_summary <- fid %>% 
  group_by(model) %>% 
  summarise( mean_fid = mean(fid), sd_fid = sd(fid) ) 
  
print(fid_summary)

# FID BOXPLOT 
 
p_fid_box <- ggplot( fid, aes( x = model, y = fid, fill = model ) ) + geom_boxplot() + theme_minimal(base_size = 14) + labs( title = "FID comparison", x = "Caption strategy", y = "FID" ) 

print(p_fid_box)

# FID HEATMAP 

p_fid_heat <- ggplot( fid, aes( x = model, y = class, fill = fid ) ) + geom_tile( color = "white" ) + geom_text( aes( label = round(fid, 1) ), size = 4 ) + theme_minimal(base_size = 14) + labs( title = "Per-class FID heatmap" ) 

print(p_fid_heat)

# ORACLE SUMMARY

oracle_summary <- oracle %>%
  group_by(model) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_f1 = mean(f1),
    mean_confidence = mean(confidence)
  )

print(oracle_summary)

# ORACLE F1 BOXPLOT

p_oracle_box <- ggplot( oracle, aes( x = model, y = f1, fill = model ) ) + geom_boxplot() + theme_minimal(base_size = 14) + labs( title = "Oracle F1-score comparison", x = "Caption strategy", y = "F1-score" ) 

print(p_oracle_box)

# ORACLE HEATMAP

p_oracle_heat <- ggplot( oracle, aes( x = model, y = class, fill = f1 ) ) + geom_tile( color = "white" ) + geom_text( aes( label = round(f1, 2) ), size = 4 ) + theme_minimal(base_size = 14) + labs( title = "Oracle F1-score heatmap" ) 

print(p_oracle_heat) 

# TOP CONFUSIONS 

top_confusions <- oracle %>% select( model, class, most_confused_with, confusion_rate ) %>% filter( confusion_rate >= 0.10 ) 

print(top_confusions) 

# KRUSKAL-WALLIS TEST 

kruskal.test( f1 ~ model, data = oracle ) 

# FID / ORACLE CORRELATION 

merged <- inner_join( oracle, fid, by = c("model", "class") ) 

cor.test( merged$fid, merged$f1, method = "spearman" )
